name: Edinburgh Tearoom Scones/Lemon Curd
recipeInstructions:
For Scones: Preheat oven to 425°F. Sift dry ingredients together. Cut in butter until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add raisins or currents. Mix buttermilk and egg together. Add all at once to flour mixture. Stir with fork just until a dough forms. Form into ball, turn onto floured surface, and gently knead 5 times until smooth.
Pat dough to about 1/2 inch thick, cut with a 2-inch cutter, and place on baking sheet 1 inch apart. Brush tops lightly with milk and sprinkle with sugar. Bake for 10 minutes or until golden brown. Serve with Lemon Curd.
For Lemon Curd: Beat eggs in heatproof bowl. Stir in remaining ingredients. Cook over simmering water, stirring frequently, until thick and smooth (about 30 minutes). Remove from heat; let cool. It will thicken further when chilled. Cover and refrigerate up to 3 weeks. This recipe has become a holiday breakfast tradition for our family, and we often give jars of Lemon Curd as gifts to friends who bake.
